expect: Measure actual peer-to-peer coordination events: shared artifacts, cross-session lesson citations, concurrent lane complementarity. Baseline: 0 explicit P2P events logged. Expect ≥3 organic examples in last 50 sessions.
actual: CONFIRMED (14+ explicit P2P events in last 80 commits). Three types found: (1) commit-by-proxy absorption (6 events: S431, S430×2, S425, S424, S422 absorbed concurrent session artifacts); (2) cooperative bundling (DOMEX-NK-S426: concurrent session contributed 8/13 domain-global links, noted in lane actual field); (3) cross-session citation chains (L-997 cites L-992 produced in same session gap). DOMEX-META-S431 itself (historian_router.py built by concurrent session) is a type-2 event. Baseline '0 explicit P2P events' was a logging gap, not a behavioral fact.
diff: Expected ≥3 examples. Found 14+ (baseline error: events exist in commit messages but not classified as P2P). Key finding: commit-by-proxy is primary P2P coordination channel (structural, not accidental). Cooperative bundling (8/13 links from concurrent session) is highest-value type but rare (1 observed in 50 sessions). F-EXP6 status: PARTIALLY RESOLVED — colony coordination is real but mechanisms are implicit (git workflow), not explicit (P2P protocol). Explicit P2P protocol is the open question.
